title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A duck boat overturned in Cambridge, injuring 11 people; it occurred on a boat ramp at 10 Museum Way.
The duck boat capsized during towing after a mechanical failure; 31 people were aboard at the time.
Boston Duck Tours halted operations for the day and is investigating the incident with authorities.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded two first-round picks to acquire 24-year-old winger JJ Peterka from the Utah Mammoth.
Peterka, who has averaged 26 goals per season, adds speed and scoring depth to the Bruins' lineup.
The Bruins prioritized Peterka's fit and potential over draft capital, aiming to strengthen their top-six forward group.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will halt coverage for weight-loss drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound starting July 1, affecting 22,000 residents.
The policy change, part of Gov. Healey's 2027 budget, aims to save $15 million annually while still covering other medical uses.
Obesity experts criticize the cuts, warning they may increase long-term healthcare costs related to untreated obesity.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins selected seven players on Day 2 of the 2026 NHL Draft in Rounds 2-7.
Boston picked two goaltenders, Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez, to enhance their future goalie pipeline.
Among the picks were forwards Nils Bartholdsson and Matvei Kotkov, noted for their scoring potential.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela resulted in 1,430 deaths, with Red Sox players expressing solidarity through messages on their hats.
Red Sox players Wilyer Abreu and Willson Contreras shared personal sentiments, highlighting their connection to their devastated homeland.
David Ortiz raised awareness and donations at Fenway Park, urging support for Venezuela's earthquake relief efforts.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized analytics, claiming they discredit players, after reports ranked him the 7th-best player on a team.
NBA insider Bobby Marks noted mixed opinions on Brown due to analytics, sparking debates on social media.
Despite trade rumors, Brown remains a hot commodity, with some teams evaluating his potential availability.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Yankees 4-1, securing a third consecutive win in the series.
Masataka Yoshida hit a leadoff home run, and Anthony Seigler made his first career MLB homer for the Red Sox.
Boston's Jake Bennett allowed only three hits in 6.1 innings, earning the win against the Yankees.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded 2021 first-round pick Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan, 23, scored 26 points in 66 games for the AHL's Colorado Eagles during the 2025-26 season.
Fabian Lysell, appearing in only 12 NHL games, had one goal and two assists during his time with the Bruins.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France beat Norway 4-1 in the 2026 World Cup at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough on Friday.
French players including Mbappe and Dembélé showcased strong offensive plays, securing France's status as World Cup entertainers.
Despite Norway's higher expected goals, France's efficient attack led to their decisive victory and tournament favoritism.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich police are investigating a specific threat by a student against two classmates at Sandwich Middle High School.
School's safety app GoGuardian flagged threatening language, prompting a quick response from school and police officials.
The student involved is not attending school pending investigation by police and the Cape & Islands District Attorney’s Office.
